revision.text:
[[image:KinKannonji.JPG|thumb|256px|金武観音寺本堂]]
'''金武観音寺'''（きんかんのんじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[金武町]]にある[[高野山真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。[[山号]]は、「金峯山」。寺社建造物の多くが[[太平洋戦争]]で消失した沖縄県下で、戦前の古い建築様式が残る貴重な木造建造物である。
== 沿革 == [[image:KinKannonji Nisshudo.JPG|thumb|256px|鍾乳洞「日秀洞」]] 伝承によれば、16世紀に日秀上人（にっしゅうしょうにん）により創建。16世紀前期、日秀上人が[[紀伊]]の国より[[唐]]を目指したが遭難して[[琉球国]]金武のフナヤ（富花港）に流れ着いた。その後、金武にある鍾乳洞を拠点とし布教活動を行い、観音寺を創建したと伝えられる。その鍾乳洞は日秀洞（にっしゅうどう）・永酒堂（えいしゅどう）と呼ばれ、現在は[[泡盛]]の酒蔵として利用されている。 [[昭和9年]]に火災により焼失した後、[[昭和17年]]に再建されたものが現存する建造物である。
== その他 == * [[昭和59年]][[6月1日]]、本堂が金武町の文化財に指定。 * [[平成3年]][[12月24日]]、[[フクギ]]が金武町の文化財に指定。
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の寺院一覧]]
[[Category:真言宗]] [[Category:金武町]] {{Buddhism-stub}}

{{出典の明記|date=2014年2月28日 (金) 10:45 (UTC)|ソートキー=沖縄きんかんのんし}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = 金峯山金武観音寺 |画像 = [[ファイル:KinKannonji.JPG|256px|金武観音寺本堂]] |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[国頭郡]][[金武町]]字金武222番地 | 緯度度 = 26|緯度分 = 27|緯度秒 = 19.15 | 経度度 = 127|経度分 = 55|経度秒 = 17.22 |山号 = 金峯山 |宗旨 = |宗派 = 高野山真言宗 |寺格 = |本尊 = 聖観音 |創建年 = |開山 = |開基 = 日秀 |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 金峯山金武観音寺 |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = }} [[ファイル:KinKannonji Nisshudo.JPG|thumb|256px|鍾乳洞「日秀洞」]] '''金武観音寺'''（きんかんのんじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[国頭郡]][[金武町]]にある[[高野山真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。[[山号]]は「金峯山」。本尊は[[聖観音]]。寺社建造物の多くが[[太平洋戦争]]で消失した沖縄県下で、戦前の古い建築様式が残る貴重な木造建造物である。
== 沿革 == 伝承によれば、[[16世紀]]に[[日秀|日秀上人]]（にっしゅうしょうにん）により創建。16世紀前期、日秀上人が[[紀伊国]]より[[唐]]を目指した（「[[補陀落渡海]]により西方浄土を目指した」との説もある）が、遭難して[[琉球王国]]金武のフナヤ（富花港）に流れ着いた。その後、金武にある[[鍾乳洞]]を拠点とし布教活動を行い、観音寺を創建したと伝えられる。その鍾乳洞は日秀洞（にっしゅうどう）・永酒堂（えいしゅどう）と呼ばれ、現在は[[泡盛]]の酒蔵として利用されている。[[昭和]]9年（[[1934年]]）に火災により焼失した後、[[昭和]]17年（[[1942年]]）に再建されたものが現存する建造物である。
== その他 == * [[昭和]]59年（[[1984年]]）[[6月1日]]、本堂が金武町の文化財に指定。 * [[平成]]3年（[[1991年]]）[[12月24日]]、境内の[[フクギ]]が金武町の文化財に指定。

{{出典の明記|date=2014年2月28日 (金) 10:45 (UTC)|ソートキー=沖縄きんかんのんし}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = 金峯山金武観音寺 |画像 = [[ファイル:KinKannonji.JPG|256px|金武観音寺本堂]] |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[国頭郡]][[金武町]]字金武222番地 | 緯度度 = 26|緯度分 = 27|緯度秒 = 19.15 | 経度度 = 127|経度分 = 55|経度秒 = 17.22 |山号 = 金峯山 |宗旨 = [[高野山真言宗]] |宗派 = |寺格 = |本尊 = 聖観音 |創建年 = |開山 = |開基 = 日秀 |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 金峯山金武観音寺 |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 = |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |地図 = 沖縄本島#Japan Okinawa }} [[ファイル:KinKannonji Nisshudo.JPG|thumb|256px|鍾乳洞「日秀洞」]] '''金武観音寺'''（きんかんのんじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[国頭郡]][[金武町]]にある[[高野山真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。[[山号]]は「金峯山」。本尊は[[聖観音]]。寺社建造物の多くが[[太平洋戦争]]で消失した沖縄県下で、戦前の古い建築様式が残る貴重な木造建造物である。
== 沿革 == 伝承によれば、[[16世紀]]に[[日秀 (真言宗)|日秀上人]]（にっしゅうしょうにん）により創建。16世紀前期、日秀上人が[[紀伊国]]より[[唐]]を目指した（「[[補陀落渡海]]により西方浄土を目指した」との説もある）が、遭難して[[琉球王国]]金武のフナヤ（富花港）に流れ着いた。その後、金武にある[[鍾乳洞]]を拠点とし布教活動を行い、観音寺を創建したと伝えられる。その鍾乳洞は日秀洞（にっしゅうどう）・永酒堂（えいしゅどう）と呼ばれ、現在は[[泡盛]]の酒蔵として利用されている。[[1934年]]（[[昭和]]9年）に火災により焼失した後、[[1942年]]（昭和17年）に再建されたものが現存する建造物である。
== その他 == * [[1984年]]（昭和59年）[[6月1日]]、本堂が金武町の文化財に指定。 * [[1991年]]（[[平成]]3年）[[12月24日]]、境内の[[フクギ]]が金武町の文化財に指定。
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の寺院一覧]] * [[金武宮]]
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kin-Kannonji}} * [http://repo.lib.hosei.ac.jp/bitstream/10114/6437/1/oki37_takahasi.pdf#search='%E9%87%91%E5%B3%B0%E5%B1%B1%E8%A6%B3%E9%9F%B3%E5%AF%BA' 補陀落渡海僧 日秀と琉球] * [http://www.town.kin.okinawa.jp/site/htdocs/midokoro/bunkazai/01.html 金武町の見どころ 金武観音寺]

revision.text:
({{ill|Henshō-ji (Okinawa)|ja|遍照寺 (沖縄市)|lt=Henshō-ji|kk=(Okinawa)}}) # {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine}} (Choju-ji Temple), and # {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|ja|長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮}} (Choraku-ji Temple) Later on all the Zenke shrines except {{ill|Yomochi Shrine and Ukishima Shrine#Ukishima Shrine|8=ja|9=世持神社|lt=Choju Shrine}} and {{ill|Choraku-ji Tenmangū|ja|長楽寺天満宮|sn|沖宮}} would convert to [[Shingon Buddhism]] and become the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|ja|琉球八社}}.
Excluded from this list is {{ill|Kin-gū|ja|金武宮}} which although quite small was always affiliated with the [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhist]] temple {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|ja|金武観音寺}} and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|ja|琉球八社}}.
{{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:History of Shinto Shrines in Okinawa forks]]
le {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|ja|金武観音寺}} and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|ja|琉球八社}}.{{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:History of Shinto Shrines in Okinawa forks]]

le {{ill|Kin Kannon-ji|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:金武観音寺]] and also joined the {{ill|Eight Ryukyu Shrines|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:琉球八社]].{{Okinawan Shrines}} [[Category:History of Shinto Shrines in Okinawa forks]]

'''金武観音寺'''（きんかんのんじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[国頭郡]][[金武町]]にある[[高野山真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。[[山号]]は「金峯山」。本尊は[[聖観音]]。寺社建造物の多くが[[太平洋戦争]]で消失した沖縄県下で、戦前の古い建築様式が残る貴重な木造建造物である。 == 沿革 == 伝承によれば、[[16世紀]]に[[日秀 (真言宗)|日秀上人]]（にっしゅうしょうにん）により創建。16世紀前期、日秀上人が[[紀伊国]]より[[唐]]を目指した（「[[補陀落渡海]]により西方浄土を目指した」との説もある）が、遭難して[[琉球王国]]金武のフナヤ（富花港）に流れ着いた。その後、金武にある[[鍾乳洞]]を拠点とし布教活動を行い、観音寺を創建したと伝えられる。その鍾乳洞は日秀洞（にっしゅうどう）と呼ばれている。[[1934年]]（[[昭和]]9年）に火災により焼失した後、[[1942年]]（昭和17年）に再建されたものが現存する建造物である。 '''金武観音寺'''（きんかんのんじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[国頭郡]][[金武町]]にある[[高野山真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。[[山号]]は「金峯山」。本尊は[[聖観音]]。寺社建造物の多くが[[太平洋戦争]]で消失した沖縄県下で、戦前の古い建築様式が残る貴重な木造建造物である。 == 沿革 == 伝承によれば、[[16世紀]]に[[日秀 (真言宗)|日秀上人]]（にっしゅうしょうにん）により創建。16世紀前期、日秀上人が[[紀伊国]]より[[唐]]を目指した（「[[補陀落渡海]]により西方浄土を目指した」との説もある）が、遭難して[[琉球王国]]金武のフナヤ（富花港）に流れ着いた。その後、金武にある[[鍾乳洞]]を拠点とし布教活動を行い、観音寺を創建したと伝えられる。その鍾乳洞は日秀洞（にっしゅうどう）と呼ばれている。[[1934]]（[[昭和]]9年）に火災により焼失した後、[[1942]]（昭和17年）に再建されたものが現存する建造物である。 == その他 == * [[1984]]（昭和59年）[[6月1日]]、本堂が金武町の文化財に指定。 * [[1991]]（[[平成]]3年）[[12月24日]]、境内の[[フクギ]]が金武町の文化財に指定。 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Kin-Kannonji}} * [[日本の寺院一覧]] * [[金武宮]] == External Links == * {{Cite {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = [[沖縄県]][[国頭郡]][[金武町]]字金武222番地 | 緯度度 = 26|緯度分 = 27|緯度秒 = 19.15 | 経度度 = 127|経度分 = 55|経度秒 = 17.22 |山号 = 金峯山 |宗旨 = [[高野山真言宗]] |宗派 = |寺格 = |本尊 = 聖観音 |創建年 = |開山 = |開基 = 日秀 |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 金峯山金武観音寺 |別称 = |札所等 = |文化財 =本堂、金武観音寺のフクギ（金武町指定文化財） |公式HP = |公式HP名 = |地図 = 沖縄本島#Japan Okinawa#Japan }} [[ファイル:KinKannonji Nisshudo.JPG|thumb|256px|鍾乳洞「日秀洞」]] '''金武観音寺'''（きんかんのんじ）は、[[沖縄県]][[国頭郡]][[金武町]]にある[[高野山真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。[[山号]]は「金峯山」。本尊は[[聖観音]]。寺社建造物の多くが[[太平洋戦争]]で消失した沖縄県下で、戦前の古い建築様式が残る貴重な木造建造物である。 == 沿革 == 伝承によれば、[[16世紀]]に[[日秀 (真言宗)|日秀上人]]（にっしゅうしょうにん）により創建。16世紀前期、日秀上人が[[紀伊国]]より[[唐]]を目指した（「[[補陀落渡海]]により西方浄土を目指した」との説もある）が、遭難して[[琉球王国]]金武のフナヤ（富花港）に流れ着いた。その後、金武にある[[鍾乳洞]]を拠点とし布教活動を行い、観音寺を創建したと伝えられる。その鍾乳洞は日秀洞（にっしゅうどう）と呼ばれている。[[1934年]]（[[昭和]]9年）に火災により焼失した後、[[1942年]]（昭和17年）に再建されたものが現存する建造物である。 == その他 == * [[1984年]]（昭和59年）[[6月1日]]、本堂が金武町の文化財に指定。 * [[1991年]]（[[平成]]3年）[[12月24日]]、境内の[[フクギ]]が金武町の文化財に指定。 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Kin-Kannonji}} * [[日本の寺院一覧]] * [[金武宮]] == 外部リンク == * {{Cite journal|和書|author=髙橋康夫 |date=2011-03 |title=補陀落渡海僧日秀上人と琉球 : 史書が創った日秀伝説 |url=https://doi.org/10.15002/00007282 |journal=沖縄文化研究= 沖縄文化研究 |ISSN=1349-4015 |publisher=法政大学沖縄文化研究所 |issue=37 |pages=1-40 |naid=120003142298 |doi=10.15002/00007282 |ref=harv}} * [http://www.town.kin.okinawa.jp/site/htdocs/midokoro/bunkazai/01.html 金武町の見どころ 金武観音寺] *[https://www.visitkintown.jp/spot/kannonji 金武町公式観光情報 観音寺] *[https://www.town.kin.okinawa.jp/guide/213/214 金武町役場 金武町指定文化財 観音寺[建設物]] {{Buddhism-stub}} {{密教2}} {{Buddhism2}} {{デフォルトソート:きんかんのんし}} [[Category:沖縄県の寺]] [[Category:高野山真言宗の寺院]] [[Category:神宮寺]] [[Category:沖縄県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:金武町の建築物]] [[Category:金武町の歴史]]

[[File:KinKannonji Nisshudo.JPG|thumb|256px|Stalactite Cave “Nichishū-dō”]]
'''Kinmu Kannon-ji''' (金武観音寺, きんかんのんじ) is a temple of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Koyasan Shingon Sect]] located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kunigami District, Okinawa|Kunigami District]], [[:en:Kin, Okinawa|Kin Town]]. Its mountain name is {{ill|Kinmōzan|ja|金峯山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmōzan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金峰山|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10563986}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Holy Kannon|ja|聖観音|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Kannon|13=User:Immanuelle/Āryāvalokiteśvara|qq=}}. In Okinawa—where many temple and shrine structures were lost during the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]—this temple is a rare surviving example of prewar traditional wooden architecture.

== History ==
According to tradition, the temple was founded in the 16th century by {{ill|Nichishū Shōnin|ja|日秀上人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū Shōnin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the early 16th century, it is said that Nichishū Shōnin set out from [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] to head for [[:en:Tang dynasty|China]] (one theory holds that he sought the Western Pure Land by means of the {{ill|Hotarakuto Kai|ja|補陀落渡海|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotarakuto Kai|qq=|1=Fudarakutokai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626847}}), but was shipwrecked and drifted to the port of Funaya (also known as Fukahaka Port) in Kin within the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]]. Thereafter, he based his missionary activities in a stalactite cave in Kin, around which he established the temple. This cave is known as {{ill|Nichishū-dō|ja|日秀洞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū-dō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The present building, which was rebuilt in 1942 (Showa 17) following a fire in 1934 (Showa 9), remains standing.

== Other ==
* In 1984 (Showa 59) on June 1, the main hall was designated as a cultural property by Kin Town.
* In 1991 (Heisei 3) on December 24, the fukugi trees within the temple grounds were designated as cultural property by Kin Town.

== References ==
{{Footnotes}}
{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==

[[File:KinKannonji Nisshudo.JPG|thumb|256px|Stalactite Cave “Nichishū-dō”]]
'''Kinmu Kannon-ji''' (金武観音寺, きんかんのんじ) is a temple of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Koyasan Shingon Sect]] located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kunigami District, Okinawa|Kunigami District]], [[:en:Kin, Okinawa|Kin Town]]. Its mountain name is {{ill|Kinmōzan|ja|金峯山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmōzan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金峰山|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10563986}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Holy Kannon|ja|聖観音|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Kannon|13=User:Immanuelle/Āryāvalokiteśvara|qq=}}. In Okinawa—where many temple and shrine structures were lost during the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]—this temple is a rare surviving example of prewar traditional wooden architecture.

== History ==
According to tradition, the temple was founded in the 16th century by {{ill|Nichishū Shōnin|ja|日秀上人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū Shōnin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the early 16th century, it is said that Nichishū Shōnin set out from [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] to head for [[:en:Tang dynasty|China]] (one theory holds that he sought the Western Pure Land by means of the {{ill|Hotarakuto Kai|ja|補陀落渡海|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotarakuto Kai|qq=|1=Fudarakutokai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626847}}), but was shipwrecked and drifted to the port of Funaya (also known as Fukahaka Port) in Kin within the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]]. Thereafter, he based his missionary activities in a stalactite cave in Kin, around which he established the temple. This cave is known as {{ill|Nichishū-dō|ja|日秀洞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū-dō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The present building, which was rebuilt in 1942 (Showa 17) following a fire in 1934 (Showa 9), remains standing.

== Other ==
* In 1984 (Showa 59) on June 1, the main hall was designated as a cultural property by Kin Town.
* In 1991 (Heisei 3) on December 24, the fukugi trees within the temple grounds were designated as cultural property by Kin Town.

[[File:KinKannonji Nisshudo.JPG|thumb|256px|Stalactite Cave “Nichishū-dō”]]
'''Kinmu Kannon-ji''' (金武観音寺, きんかんのんじ) is a temple of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Koyasan Shingon Sect]] located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kunigami District, Okinawa|Kunigami District]], [[:en:Kin, Okinawa|Kin Town]]. Its mountain name is {{ill|Kinmōzan|ja|金峯山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmōzan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金峰山|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10563986}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Holy Kannon|ja|聖観音|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Kannon|13=User:Immanuelle/Āryāvalokiteśvara|qq=}}. In Okinawa—where many temple and shrine structures were lost during the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]—this temple is a rare surviving example of prewar traditional wooden architecture.

== History ==
According to tradition, the temple was founded in the 16th century by {{ill|Nichishū Shōnin|ja|日秀上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū Shōnin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the early 16th century, it is said that Nichishū Shōnin set out from [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] to head for [[:en:Tang dynasty|China]] (one theory holds that he sought the Western Pure Land by means of the {{ill|Hotarakuto Kai|ja|補陀落渡海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotarakuto Kai|1=Fudarakutokai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626847}}), but was shipwrecked and drifted to the port of Funaya (also known as Fukahaka Port) in Kin within the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]]. Thereafter, he based his missionary activities in a stalactite cave in Kin, around which he established the temple. This cave is known as {{ill|Nichishū-dō|ja|日秀洞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The present building, which was rebuilt in 1942 (Showa 17) following a fire in 1934 (Showa 9), remains standing.

== Other ==
* In 1984 (Showa 59) on June 1, the main hall was designated as a cultural property by Kin Town.
* In 1991 (Heisei 3) on December 24, the fukugi trees within the temple grounds were designated as cultural property by Kin Town.

[[File:KinKannonji Nisshudo.JPG|thumb|256px|Stalactite Cave “Nichishū-dō”]]
'''Kinmu Kannon-ji''' (金武観音寺, きんかんのんじ) is a temple of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Koyasan Shingon Sect]] located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kunigami District, Okinawa|Kunigami District]], [[:en:Kin, Okinawa|Kin Town]]. Its mountain name is {{ill|Kinmōzan|ja|金峯山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmōzan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金峰山|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10563986}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Holy Kannon|ja|聖観音|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Kannon|13=User:Immanuelle/Āryāvalokiteśvara|qq=}}. In Okinawa—where many temple and shrine structures were lost during the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]—this temple is a rare surviving example of prewar traditional wooden architecture.

== History ==
According to tradition, the temple was founded in the 16th century by {{ill|Nichishū Shōnin|ja|日秀上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū Shōnin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the early 16th century, it is said that Nichishū Shōnin set out from [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] to head for [[:en:Tang dynasty|China]] (one theory holds that he sought the Western Pure Land by means of the {{ill|Hotarakuto Kai|ja|補陀落渡海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotarakuto Kai|1=Fudarakutokai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626847}}), but was shipwrecked and drifted to the port of Funaya (also known as Fukahaka Port) in Kin within the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]]. Thereafter, he based his missionary activities in a stalactite cave in Kin, around which he established the temple. This cave is known as {{ill|Nichishū-dō|ja|日秀洞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The present building, which was rebuilt in 1942 (Showa 17) following a fire in 1934 (Showa 9), remains standing.

== Other ==
* In 1984 (Showa 59) on June 1, the main hall was designated as a cultural property by Kin Town.
* In 1991 (Heisei 3) on December 24, the fukugi trees within the temple grounds were designated as cultural property by Kin Town.

[[File:KinKannonji Nisshudo.JPG|thumb|256px|Stalactite Cave “Nichishū-dō”]]
'''Kinmu Kannon-ji''' (金武観音寺, きんかんのんじ) is a temple of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Koyasan Shingon Sect]] located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kunigami District, Okinawa|Kunigami District]], [[:en:Kin, Okinawa|Kin Town]]. Its mountain name is {{ill|Kinmōzan|ja|金峯山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmōzan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金峰山|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10563986}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Holy Kannon|ja|聖観音|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Kannon|13=User:Immanuelle/Āryāvalokiteśvara|qq=}}. In Okinawa—where many temple and shrine structures were lost during the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]—this temple is a rare surviving example of prewar traditional wooden architecture.

== History ==
According to tradition, the temple was founded in the 16th century by {{ill|Nichishū Shōnin|ja|日秀上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū Shōnin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the early 16th century, it is said that Nichishū Shōnin set out from [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] to head for [[:en:Tang dynasty|China]] (one theory holds that he sought the Western Pure Land by means of the {{ill|Hotarakuto Kai|ja|補陀落渡海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotarakuto Kai|1=Fudarakutokai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626847}}), but was shipwrecked and drifted to the port of Funaya (also known as Fukahaka Port) in Kin within the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]]. Thereafter, he based his missionary activities in a stalactite cave in Kin, around which he established the temple. This cave is known as {{ill|Nichishū-dō|ja|日秀洞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The present building, which was rebuilt in 1942 (Showa 17) following a fire in 1934 (Showa 9), remains standing.

== Other ==
* In 1984 (Showa 59) on June 1, the main hall was designated as a cultural property by Kin Town.
* In 1991 (Heisei 3) on December 24, the fukugi trees within the temple grounds were designated as cultural property by Kin Town.

[[File:KinKannonji Nisshudo.JPG|thumb|256px|Stalactite Cave “Nichishū-dō”]]
'''Kinmu Kannon-ji''' (金武観音寺, きんかんのんじ) is a temple of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Koyasan Shingon Sect]] located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kunigami District, Okinawa|Kunigami District]], [[:en:Kin, Okinawa|Kin Town]]. Its mountain name is {{ill|Kinmōzan|ja|金峯山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmōzan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金峰山|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10563986}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Holy Kannon|ja|聖観音|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Kannon|13=User:Immanuelle/Āryāvalokiteśvara|qq=}}. In Okinawa—where many temple and shrine structures were lost during the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]—this temple is a rare surviving example of prewar traditional wooden architecture.

== History ==
According to tradition, the temple was founded in the 16th century by {{ill|Nichishū Shōnin|ja|日秀上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū Shōnin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the early 16th century, it is said that Nichishū Shōnin set out from [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] to head for [[:en:Tang dynasty|China]] (one theory holds that he sought the Western Pure Land by means of the {{ill|Hotarakuto Kai|ja|補陀落渡海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotarakuto Kai|1=Fudarakutokai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626847}}), but was shipwrecked and drifted to the port of Funaya (also known as Fukahaka Port) in Kin within the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]]. Thereafter, he based his missionary activities in a stalactite cave in Kin, around which he established the temple. This cave is known as {{ill|Nichishū-dō|ja|日秀洞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The present building, which was rebuilt in 1942 (Showa 17) following a fire in 1934 (Showa 9), remains standing.

== Other ==
* In 1984 (Showa 59) on June 1, the main hall was designated as a cultural property by Kin Town.
* In 1991 (Heisei 3) on December 24, the fukugi trees within the temple grounds were designated as cultural property by Kin Town.

[[File:KinKannonji Nisshudo.JPG|thumb|256px|Stalactite Cave “Nichishū-dō”]]
'''Kinmu Kannon-ji''' (金武観音寺, きんかんのんじ) is a temple of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Koyasan Shingon Sect]] located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kunigami District, Okinawa|Kunigami District]], [[:en:Kin, Okinawa|Kin Town]]. Its mountain name is {{ill|Kinmōzan|ja|金峯山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmōzan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金峰山|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10563986}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Holy Kannon|ja|聖観音|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Kannon|13=User:Immanuelle/Āryāvalokiteśvara|qq=}}. In Okinawa—where many temple and shrine structures were lost during the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]—this temple is a rare surviving example of prewar traditional wooden architecture.

== History ==
According to tradition, the temple was founded in the 16th century by {{ill|Nichishū Shōnin|ja|日秀上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū Shōnin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the early 16th century, it is said that Nichishū Shōnin set out from [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] to head for [[:en:Tang dynasty|China]] (one theory holds that he sought the Western Pure Land by means of the {{ill|Hotarakuto Kai|ja|補陀落渡海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotarakuto Kai|1=Fudarakutokai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626847}}), but was shipwrecked and drifted to the port of Funaya (also known as Fukahaka Port) in Kin within the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]]. Thereafter, he based his missionary activities in a stalactite cave in Kin, around which he established the temple. This cave is known as {{ill|Nichishū-dō|ja|日秀洞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The present building, which was rebuilt in 1942 (Showa 17) following a fire in 1934 (Showa 9), remains standing.

== Other ==
* In 1984 (Showa 59) on June 1, the main hall was designated as a cultural property by Kin Town.
* In 1991 (Heisei 3) on December 24, the fukugi trees within the temple grounds were designated as cultural property by Kin Town.

'''Kinmu Kannon-ji''' (金武観音寺, きんかんのんじ) is a temple of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Koyasan Shingon Sect]] located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Kunigami District, Okinawa|Kunigami District]], [[:en:Kin, Okinawa|Kin Town]]. Its mountain name is {{ill|Kinmōzan|ja|金峯山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmōzan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金峰山|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10563986}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Holy Kannon|ja|聖観音|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Kannon|13=User:Immanuelle/Āryāvalokiteśvara|qq=}}. In Okinawa—where many temple and shrine structures were lost during the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]—this temple is a rare surviving example of prewar traditional wooden architecture.

== History ==
According to tradition, the temple was founded in the 16th century by {{ill|Nichishū Shōnin|ja|日秀上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū Shōnin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the early 16th century, it is said that Nichishū Shōnin set out from [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]] to head for [[:en:Tang dynasty|China]] (one theory holds that he sought the Western Pure Land by means of the {{ill|Hotarakuto Kai|ja|補陀落渡海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotarakuto Kai|1=Fudarakutokai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626847}}), but was shipwrecked and drifted to the port of Funaya (also known as Fukahaka Port) in Kin within the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]]. Thereafter, he based his missionary activities in a stalactite cave in Kin, around which he established the temple. This cave is known as {{ill|Nichishū-dō|ja|日秀洞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The present building, which was rebuilt in 1942 (Showa 17) following a fire in 1934 (Showa 9), remains standing.

== Other ==
* In 1984 (Showa 59) on June 1, the main hall was designated as a cultural property by Kin Town.
* In 1991 (Heisei 3) on December 24, the fukugi trees within the temple grounds were designated as cultural property by Kin Town.

[[File:KinKannonji Nisshudo.JPG|thumb|256px|Stalactite Cave “Nichishū-dō”]]
'''Kinmu Kannon-ji''' (金武観音寺, きんかんのんじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|WD=Q3077357|lt=Koyasan Shingon Sect}} located in {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kunigami District, Okinawa|en|Kunigami District, Okinawa|WD=Q1330132|lt=Kunigami District}}, {{ill|Kin, Okinawa|en|Kin, Okinawa|WD=Q1350738|lt=Kin Town}}. Its mountain name is {{ill|Kinmōzan|ja|金峯山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmōzan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金峰山|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10563986}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Holy Kannon|ja|聖観音|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Kannon|13=User:Immanuelle/Āryāvalokiteśvara|qq=}}. In Okinawa—where many temple and shrine structures were lost during the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}—this temple is a rare surviving example of prewar traditional wooden architecture.

== History ==
According to tradition, the temple was founded in the 16th century by {{ill|Nichishū Shōnin|ja|日秀上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū Shōnin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the early 16th century, it is said that Nichishū Shōnin set out from {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province}} to head for {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=China}} (one theory holds that he sought the Western Pure Land by means of the {{ill|Hotarakuto Kai|ja|補陀落渡海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotarakuto Kai|1=Fudarakutokai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626847}}), but was shipwrecked and drifted to the port of Funaya (also known as Fukahaka Port) in Kin within the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom}}. Thereafter, he based his missionary activities in a stalactite cave in Kin, around which he established the temple. This cave is known as {{ill|Nichishū-dō|ja|日秀洞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The present building, which was rebuilt in 1942 (Showa 17) following a fire in 1934 (Showa 9), remains standing.

== Other ==
* In 1984 (Showa 59) on June 1, the main hall was designated as a cultural property by Kin Town.
* In 1991 (Heisei 3) on December 24, the fukugi trees within the temple grounds were designated as cultural property by Kin Town.

[[File:KinKannonji Nisshudo.JPG|thumb|256px|Stalactite Cave “Nichishū-dō”]]
'''Kinmu Kannon-ji''' (金武観音寺, きんかんのんじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|WD=Q3077357|lt=Koyasan Shingon Sect|1=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}} located in {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kunigami District, Okinawa|en|Kunigami District, Okinawa|WD=Q1330132|lt=Kunigami District|1=Kunigami District, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Kin, Okinawa|en|Kin, Okinawa|WD=Q1350738|lt=Kin Town|1=Kin, Okinawa}}. Its mountain name is {{ill|Kinmōzan|ja|金峯山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmōzan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金峰山|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10563986}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Holy Kannon|ja|聖観音|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Kannon|13=User:Immanuelle/Āryāvalokiteśvara|qq=}}. In Okinawa—where many temple and shrine structures were lost during the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}—this temple is a rare surviving example of prewar traditional wooden architecture.

== History ==
According to tradition, the temple was founded in the 16th century by {{ill|Nichishū Shōnin|ja|日秀上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū Shōnin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the early 16th century, it is said that Nichishū Shōnin set out from {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}} to head for {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=China|1=Tang dynasty}} (one theory holds that he sought the Western Pure Land by means of the {{ill|Hotarakuto Kai|ja|補陀落渡海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotarakuto Kai|1=Fudaraku tokai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626847}}), but was shipwrecked and drifted to the port of Funaya (also known as Fukahaka Port) in Kin within the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom|1=Ryukyu Kingdom}}. Thereafter, he based his missionary activities in a stalactite cave in Kin, around which he established the temple. This cave is known as {{ill|Nichishū-dō|ja|日秀洞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The present building, which was rebuilt in 1942 (Showa 17) following a fire in 1934 (Showa 9), remains standing.

== Other ==
* In 1984 (Showa 59) on June 1, the main hall was designated as a cultural property by Kin Town.
* In 1991 (Heisei 3) on December 24, the fukugi trees within the temple grounds were designated as cultural property by Kin Town.

[[File:KinKannonji Nisshudo.JPG|thumb|256px|Stalactite Cave “Nichishū-dō”]]
'''Kinmu Kannon-ji''' (金武観音寺, きんかんのんじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|WD=Q3077357|lt=Koyasan Shingon Sect|1=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}} located in {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kunigami District, Okinawa|en|Kunigami District, Okinawa|WD=Q1330132|lt=Kunigami District|1=Kunigami District, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Kin, Okinawa|en|Kin, Okinawa|WD=Q1350738|lt=Kin Town|1=Kin, Okinawa}}. Its mountain name is {{ill|Kinmōzan|ja|金峯山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmōzan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金峰山|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10563986}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Holy Kannon|ja|聖観音|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Kannon|13=User:Immanuelle/Āryāvalokiteśvara|qq=}}. In Okinawa—where many temple and shrine structures were lost during the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}—this temple is a rare surviving example of prewar traditional wooden architecture.

== History ==
According to tradition, the temple was founded in the 16th century by {{ill|Nichishū Shōnin|ja|日秀上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū Shōnin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the early 16th century, it is said that Nichishū Shōnin set out from {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}} to head for {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=China|1=Tang dynasty}} (one theory holds that he sought the Western Pure Land by means of the {{ill|Hotarakuto Kai|ja|補陀落渡海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotarakuto Kai|1=Fudaraku tokai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626847}}), but was shipwrecked and drifted to the port of Funaya (also known as Fukahaka Port) in Kin within the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom|1=Ryukyu Kingdom}}. Thereafter, he based his missionary activities in a stalactite cave in Kin, around which he established the temple. This cave is known as {{ill|Nichishū-dō|ja|日秀洞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The present building, which was rebuilt in 1942 (Showa 17) following a fire in 1934 (Showa 9), remains standing.

== Other ==
* In 1984 (Showa 59) on June 1, the main hall was designated as a cultural property by Kin Town.
* In 1991 (Heisei 3) on December 24, the fukugi trees within the temple grounds were designated as cultural property by Kin Town.

[[File:KinKannonji Nisshudo.JPG|thumb|256px|Stalactite Cave “Nichishū-dō”]]
'''Kinmu Kannon-ji''' (金武観音寺, きんかんのんじ) is a temple of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qid=Q3077357|lt=Koyasan Shingon Sect|1=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}} located in {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|qid=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kunigami District, Okinawa|en|Kunigami District, Okinawa|qid=Q1330132|lt=Kunigami District|1=Kunigami District, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Kin, Okinawa|en|Kin, Okinawa|qid=Q1350738|lt=Kin Town|1=Kin, Okinawa}}. Its mountain name is {{ill|Kinmōzan|ja|金峯山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmōzan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金峰山|1=Mount Kinpusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10563986}}, and its principal image is {{ill|Holy Kannon|ja|聖観音|1=Āryāvalokiteśvara|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holy Kannon|13=User:Immanuelle/Āryāvalokiteśvara|qq=}}. In Okinawa—where many temple and shrine structures were lost during the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}—this temple is a rare surviving example of prewar traditional wooden architecture.

== History ==
According to tradition, the temple was founded in the 16th century by {{ill|Nichishū Shōnin|ja|日秀上人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū Shōnin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the early 16th century, it is said that Nichishū Shōnin set out from {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}} to head for {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|qid=Q9683|lt=China|1=Tang dynasty}} (one theory holds that he sought the Western Pure Land by means of the {{ill|Hotarakuto Kai|ja|補陀落渡海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotarakuto Kai|1=Fudaraku tokai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11626847}}), but was shipwrecked and drifted to the port of Funaya (also known as Fukahaka Port) in Kin within the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|qid=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom|1=Ryukyu Kingdom}}. Thereafter, he based his missionary activities in a stalactite cave in Kin, around which he established the temple. This cave is known as {{ill|Nichishū-dō|ja|日秀洞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishū-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The present building, which was rebuilt in 1942 (Showa 17) following a fire in 1934 (Showa 9), remains standing.

== Other ==
* In 1984 (Showa 59) on June 1, the main hall was designated as a cultural property by Kin Town.
* In 1991 (Heisei 3) on December 24, the fukugi trees within the temple grounds were designated as cultural property by Kin Town.
